model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the safer pick because it has the much stronger overall two-way profile (KenPom/Torvik both #2) with an elite defense that can better withstand Alabama’s high-variance, pace-and-shooting driven attack. Alabama’s offense is good enough to win this outright (top-3 AdjO on both systems), but its defense grades far behind Michigan’s, which is a tough way to live if Michigan controls shot quality and the glass. The upset path is real if Alabama turns it into a track meet and wins the 3-point math, but Michigan’s defensive floor and higher overall efficiency margin make it the likelier Sweet 16 survivor.

reasoningStep.title: Sweet 16: Michigan vs Alabama matchup call
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan’s elite defense and superior overall efficiency edge outweigh Alabama’s explosive offense, though Alabama’s 3-point/tempo variance keeps upset risk meaningful.
evidence: Ratings snapshot: Michigan (KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38; Torvik #2) vs Alabama (KenPom #16, AdjEM +25.64; Torvik #17).
Unit strengths: Alabama offense is elite (KenPom/Torvik AdjO rank #3), but its defense is comparatively vulnerable (KenPom AdjD 103.4; Torvik defensive rank #67).

Matchup logic: Michigan’s top-tier defense (KenPom AdjD 88.4; Torvik defensive rank #2) provides a more reliable floor in a one-game Sweet 16 setting, while Alabama’s main upset path is pace + 3-point variance.
News/availability: No clearly verified, matchup-flipping Michigan/Alabama injury update surfaced in the quick news searches to override the baseline efficiency edge.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.title: NCAA Sweet 16 Injury Report Today: Latest Updates on Sam ... - NewsBreak
result.snippet: Chris Moore - Auburn Auburn senior forward Chris Moore sprained his ankle in the first-round victory over Alabama State on March 22. After sitting out the second-round win against Creighton, Moore returned to full practice on Tuesday and Thursday, with coach Bruce Pearl confirming his likely availability against Michigan .

result.title: Men's March Madness Injury Tracker: Which Teams Are Missing Key Players ...
result.snippet: Most Sweet 16 teams like Michigan State, Tennessee, Alabama , Florida, Purdue, Maryland, Arizona, Michigan , BYU, and Ole Miss report no major injuries. Ole Miss advanced after Iowa State's Keshon Gilbert, a 13.4-point scorer, missed their game with a groin injury . For now, the field looks mostly fit for a fierce round starting March 27.

result.text: NCAA Sweet 16 Injury Report Today: Latest Updates on Sam Walters, Chris Moore and More - NewsBreak Open in NewsBreak | Sign in Choose your location See all locations Customize your news Local News Headlines Posts Newsletter Topics Reactions Sign In About NewsBreak About Us Careers Help Center Contact Blog Partner with us Publishers Advertisers Contributors Local Business Terms of Use Privacy Policy Don’t Sell My Info Community Standards Follow us ©2025 NewsBreak. All Rights Reserved. 17K Posts 29K Followers College Sports Network Website College Sports Network covers all of the major college sports, including football, men's basketball, women's basketball, and more. collegefootballnetwork.com Basketball NCAA Sweet 16 Injury Report Today: Latest Updates on Sam Walters, Chris Moore and More By Ravi Shankar , 2025-03-28 As the Sweet 16 tips off tonight, a handful of key injuries could shape the outcomes of these critical matchups. With four games on tap, teams are making final preparations while managing player health concerns. From Kentucky’s season-ending setback to Auburn’s multiple minor ailments, medical updates will be closely monitored as tipoff approaches. Let’s examine the latest injury news as teams battle for Elite Eight berths. College Sports Network’s CBB Power Rankings analyze every team’s strength in a proprietary ranking system, from No. 1 to No. 364. Who are the real contenders? Friday’s Injury Report Jaxson Robinson – Kentucky Kentucky’s championship hopes took a devastating hit when senior guard Jaxson Robinson suffered a right wrist fracture against LSU on February 25. The 6’6″ BYU transfer, who averaged 13.0 points while shooting 37.8% from deep, underwent surgery on March 1 and is definitively out for the season. Robinson’s absence leaves a significant void in Kentucky’s perimeter scoring ahead of their clash with Tennessee. The Wildcats have relied on Koby Brea (15.5 PPG in tournament play) and freshman Collin Chandler to fill the gap, managing a 2-0 record against the Volunteers this season despite missing Robinson in their second matchup. Sam Walters – Michigan Michigan forward Sam Walters remains sidelined for the Sweet 16 with a lower back disc injury that has kept him out since February 8. The 6’10” sophomore sharpshooter hasn’t played in Michigan’s last 13 games, including their entire NCAA Tournament run. Before his injury, Walters averaged 5.8 PPG and 2.9 RPG while shooting 38.1% from three in 12.7 minutes per game. His absence has hurt Michigan’s perimeter shooting forcing coach Dusty May to rely more heavily on frontcourt players Wolf and Goldin against Auburn’s athletic lineup. Chris Moore – Auburn Auburn senior forward Chris Moore sprained his ankle in the first-round victory over Alabama State on March 22. After sitting out the second-round win against Creighton, Moore returned to full practice on Tuesday and Thursday, with coach Bruce Pearl confirming his likely availability against Michigan. Despite li

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
